title: Ophthalmologic Emergencies

vignette.text: A 70-year-old with AFib presents with sudden painless vision loss in right eye 45 minutes ago. VA is hand motion only.

physicalExam.fundus: Pale retina with cherry-red spot at macula

questions.question: Most likely diagnosis?
questions.explanation: CRAO: sudden painless vision loss, pale retina, cherry-red spot (fovea has separate blood supply). Often embolic from carotid or cardiac source.

keyTakeaways: CRAO: 90-minute window, ocular massage, lower IOP
Acute angle closure: mid-dilated pupil, halos, IOP >40
Chemical burns: immediate copious irrigation FIRST
Retinal detachment: flashes, floaters, curtain over vision
Globe rupture: shield eye, no pressure, emergent surgery
